concept.title: GUIDES-19555: Make new reltable easier to use (column collapse, tags view)
concept.shortdesc: In AEM Guides Author with tags enabled, a new map reltable without a header row can look like a four-cell grid until a topic is placed in the first cell; then later columns collapse and targets are hard to add. Full tags view can also garble overlapping reltable link text.

section.title: Problem
section.p: Customers (Workday, LinkedIn, Thomson Reuters, and others per Jira) report reltable authoring pain in the map editor when tagging is enabled:

ul.li: Column collapse: After creating a new reltable without a header row and placing a reference in the first cell, the second (and later) columns shrink so much that placing a target reference is very difficult (repro noted by engineering).
Full tags view: Reltable link text can overlap adjacent columns, so authors fall back to Source view to fix or extend relationships.
Workday — IMS org 0AB2768154D414A20A4C98A5@AdobeOrg, program cm-p108955, environment e1062829; reproducible on prod and stage.
Historical: AEM Guides 2024.6.0.72 cited during support triage.
Dynamics / investigations linked from Jira (e.g. E-001341872, full investigation URL in ticket).

ol.li: Open AEM Guides Author view with tags enabled.
Create a new reltable without a header row in a map.
Observe an initial four-cell grid layout.
Place a topic reference in the first cell.
Observe the second column collapsing (last columns nearly zero width), hindering target placement.

strow.stentry: The reltable keeps a usable grid so target topics can be placed in subsequent cells.
Column width collapses after the first-cell link; full tags view can overlap link text across columns.

section.title: Bundled minimal DITA (this dataset)
section.p: File GUIDES-19555-sample-map.ditamap contains a reltable with no relheader, two relrow elements, two relcell children each (2×2 grid). The first cell references GUIDES-19555-topic-source.dita; other cells are empty for you to populate in the editor when testing.

section.title: Verification notes (Jira comments)
section.p: Engineering reported not reproducible on the new editor (see Manav Mittal comment, Jan 2026). Prashant Pal verified on build 5.3.0.6523 with a sample map (screenshot in Jira, Mar 2026). Issue status in Jira is Closed; this bundle remains useful as a structural repro dataset for reltable layout and regression checks.
